_meta.method: Cross-referenced Soto Zen Buddhist Association directory (szba.org), Centro Zen de México site (sites.google.com/site/czenmx), Zen México (Kosen Sangha — zenmexico.org), Asociación Budista Zen Deshimaru dojo list (zen-deshimaru.com), Mar de Jade retreat centre listing, Casa Zen México social presence, plus the Plum Village Latin America tour records. Each entry verified against an official lineage organisation or the centre's own site.
_meta.coverageEstimate: Medium-high (~70%) for organised Zen sanghas with a website. Strong coverage of White Plum (Maezumi/Tesshin) lineage, Kosen/Deshimaru dojos, and Mar de Jade retreats. Plum Village home sanghas, Dharma Sangha (Baker Roshi) Cuernavaca and Dhammapada-Xochimilco group included where sites confirm. Ejo Takata's Rinzai zazenkai legacy survives only as informal groups and is not separately listed.
knownGaps: Ejo Takata zazenkai (Rinzai legacy in CDMX) has no current standalone website
Several Plum Village home sanghas in Mexico City and Guadalajara not in plumline.org directory in a citable form
No active Kwan Um, Sanbō Zen International, or Ōbaku presence located as of 2026
Centro Zen del Escorpión Negro and Centro Zen Maezumi referenced in directories but lack verified current sites

places.name: El Centro Zen de México, A.R.
places.lineage: Sōtō / White Plum (Maezumi Rōshi → Tesshin Sanderson)
places.address: Calle G Manzana XIII No. 18, Col. Educación, 04400 Ciudad de México, CDMX
places.notes: Established when Maezumi Rōshi sent senior disciple Tesshin Sanderson as Zen instructor in 1986; Tesshin received Dharma Transmission and was named Zen Master in 1991. SZBA-affiliated.

places.name: Dōjō Zen Mon Kō — Zen México
places.address: Ingres 127, Col. Nonoalco, Alcaldía Benito Juárez, 03710 Ciudad de México, CDMX
places.notes: Main dōjō of the Zen México non-profit (Kosen Sangha / Taisen Deshimaru lineage).

places.name: Grupo Zen Monterrey — Zen México
places.address: Ángela Peralta 142A, María Luisa, Centro, 64049 Monterrey, N.L.
places.notes: Monterrey practice group of the Kosen Sangha network.

places.name: Mar de Jade — Silent Zen Meditation Retreat Center
places.lineage: Sōtō / Chan (bilingual sesshin host; Laura del Valle)
places.address: Chacala, Riviera Nayarit (90 min north of Puerto Vallarta), Mexico
places.notes: Has hosted annual Zen and Chan sesshins for 30+ years; founded by long-time Zen practitioner Dr. Laura del Valle.

places.name: Casa Zen México
places.address: Dr. Vértiz 1418, Col. Vértiz Narvarte, 03310 Ciudad de México, CDMX
places.notes: Lay Zen practice community in Coyoacán. Primary online presence is Facebook.

places.name: Dharma Sangha México
places.lineage: Sōtō (Suzuki Rōshi → Richard Baker Rōshi lineage)
places.notes: Mexican branch of Richard Baker Rōshi's Dharma Sangha (Crestone, CO / Johanneshof). Cuernavaca residential practice.

places.name: Dhammapada Budismo Zen — Dōjō Zen México
places.notes: Weekly Zen practice and seminars open to public; located in Xochimilco, CDMX.
